summary: Writes POP contents to a single file or a numbered file sequence. Format determines which attributes survive the export.

key_params.name: file
key_params.note: Output path; use $F4 for sequence numbering

key_params.name: format
key_params.note: obj, ply, exr, spz — .obj/.spz have fixed attribute sets, .exr/.ply support arbitrary attributes

key_params.name: record
key_params.note: Record on/off; pulse for one-shot save

key_params.name: frame
key_params.note: Frame number used in the sequence pattern

common_gotchas: If you need every POP attribute round-tripped, use .ply or .exr — .obj/.spz drop anything outside their fixed schemas.
For Alembic-style animated geometry use Alembic Out POP instead — File Out POP writes per-frame snapshots, Alembic packs them in one container.
